starting_milestone: Driver-out validation on closed course (no safety driver, up to 65 mph)
ending_milestone: Targeted commercial launch (driverless freight hauling for Daimler Truck network)
notes_on_comparability: Majority-owned by Daimler Truck since 2019 -- OEM ownership and captive commercialization channel make this timeline faster than an independently-capitalized startup would likely achieve. Do not average with independent-startup timelines without adjustment.

ending_milestone: First fully humanless commercial truckload (Houston-Dallas, no safety driver / in-cab observer / remote fallback)
source: FreightWaves / Road to Autonomy field report, 2026-04-30, https://www.roadtoautonomy.com/bot-auto-commercial-launch-field-report/
notes_on_comparability: Independently capitalized ($40M raised per press coverage) rather than OEM-backed, a useful contrast case to Torc. The spec-referenced 30-truck (2026) / 100-truck-breakeven (2027) fleet targets could not be independently verified in this pass (see source_registry.csv BOTAUTO-003/004) -- treat only as unverified scenario framing.

starting_milestone: Emerged from stealth with $80M seed + Series A
ending_milestone: Targeted first operator-less deployments with customers
notes_on_comparability: IMPORTANT SEGMENT NOTE: Bedrock Robotics builds autonomy retrofits for excavators (autonomous CONSTRUCTION equipment), not autonomous trucks, despite the trucking-adjacent framing in the project spec's problem statement (section 1.4). Classified here under 'construction,' not 'autonomous_trucking' -- do not blend its dwell times into the trucking segment default.

starting_date: 2024 (year only, partnership start per Kubota's own retrospective framing)
ending_milestone: Kubota equity investment + CES 2026 demo + early commercial deployment (few units in pilot with real customers, western US)
notes_on_comparability: No unit count disclosed for the 'early commercial deployment' -- described qualitatively only. Distribution-channel model (via Kubota dealers) differs from direct-sales deployment motions elsewhere in this table.

ending_milestone: Exited stealth with $450M Series A and flagship manufacturing pilot result
notes_on_comparability: Flagship pilot cycle-time result (sub-2-minutes/cycle) is self-reported by the company, not third-party verified. Rhoda licenses a software/model layer (FutureVision) rather than deploying owned hardware fleets, so its 'deployment' timeline is not directly comparable to hardware-fleet operators without adjustment.

starting_milestone: GreenBox joint-venture Framework Agreement signed with SoftBank
ending_milestone: FY2025 10-K discloses ~$22.5B total backlog; GreenBox committed to $7.5B of purchases over 6 years
notes_on_comparability: This is a BACKLOG figure, not recognized revenue -- see spec section 8.5 / section 14. GreenBox is a 35%(Symbotic)/65%(SoftBank) joint venture rather than a fully independent commercial counterparty, which matters when interpreting 'largest customer' concentration.
